questions.text: Which of the following is the smallest negative integer?
questions.explanation: There is no smallest negative integer as negative numbers extend infinitely. For any negative integer, there's always a smaller one.

questions.text: Zero is greater than every negative integer.
questions.explanation: Zero is greater than all negative integers and less than all positive integers. It is neither positive nor negative.

questions.text: What is the absolute value of -25?
questions.explanation: Absolute value is the distance from zero on the number line. |−25| = 25, as it is 25 units away from zero.
The additive inverse of a number is what you add to get zero. 7 + (-7) = 0, so the additive inverse of 7 is -7.
